# Rosalind Problem: Computing_GC_Content (C Solution)

## Problem Understanding

Given: At most 10 DNA strings in FASTA format (of length at most 1 kbp each).
Return: The ID of the string having the highest GC-content, followed by the GC-content of that string.

## Solution Approach

1. Parse FASTA format to extract DNA sequences
2. Calculate GC-content for each sequence
3. Find the sequence with maximum GC-content
4. Output the result in required format

## C Implementation

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#define MAX_ID_LEN 100
#define MAX_SEQ_LEN 1000

// Structure to store sequence information
typedef struct {
    char id[MAX_ID_LEN];
    char sequence[MAX_SEQ_LEN];
    double gc_content;
} DNASequence;

// Function to calculate GC content
double calculate_gc_content(const char* sequence) {
    int gc_count = 0;
    int total_count = 0;
    
    for (int i = 0; sequence[i] != '\0'; i++) {
        char nucleotide = sequence[i];
        if (nucleotide == 'G' || nucleotide == 'C') {
            gc_count++;
        }
        if (nucleotide == 'A' || nucleotide == 'T' || nucleotide == 'G' || nucleotide == 'C') {
            total_count++;
        }
    }
    
    if (total_count == 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    
    return (double)gc_count / total_count * 100.0;
}

// Function to read FASTA format and parse sequences
int read_fasta_sequences(DNASequence* sequences, int max_sequences) {
    char line[2000];
    int count = 0;
    
    while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), stdin) != NULL && count < max_sequences) {
        // Skip empty lines
        if (line[0] == '\n' || line[0] == '\r') {
            continue;
        }
        
        // Check if it's an ID line (starts with >)
        if (line[0] == '>') {
            // Extract ID
            line[strcspn(line, "\n")] = '\0';  // Remove newline
            strcpy(sequences[count].id, line + 1);  // Skip the '>'
            sequences[count].sequence[0] = '\0';  // Initialize sequence
            count++;
        } else {
            // This is a sequence line
            if (count > 0) {
                // Append to previous sequence
                int len = strlen(sequences[count-1].sequence);
                strcpy(sequences[count-1].sequence + len, line);
                // Remove newline from sequence line
                sequences[count-1].sequence[strcspn(sequences[count-1].sequence, "\n")] = '\0';
            }
        }
    }
    
    return count;
}

int main() {
    DNASequence sequences[10];  // Maximum 10 sequences as per problem
    int num_sequences = read_fasta_sequences(sequences, 10);
    
    // Calculate GC content for each sequence
    for (int i = 0; i < num_sequences; i++) {
        sequences[i].gc_content = calculate_gc_content(sequences[i].sequence);
    }
    
    // Find sequence with maximum GC content
    int max_index = 0;
    double max_gc = sequences[0].gc_content;
    
    for (int i = 1; i < num_sequences; i++) {
        if (sequences[i].gc_content > max_gc) {
            max_gc = sequences[i].gc_content;
            max_index = i;
        }
    }
    
    // Output result
    printf("%s\n", sequences[max_index].id);
    printf("%.6f\n", max_gc);
    
    return 0;
}
```

## How to Compile and Run

```bash
gcc -o gc_content gc_content.c
./gc_content < input.txt
```

## Example Input Format

```
>Rosalind_6404
